1.title: A prophet's marriage as a sign
1.text: Hosea's marriage to Gomer was itself a message: her unfaithfulness pictured Israel departing from the Lord, and the names of their children became living sermons the whole nation could hear.

1.question: Why did the Lord tell Hosea to name the first son Jezreel (Hosea 1:4)?
options: He would avenge the blood of Jezreel upon the house of Jehu
It was the king's favorite valley

1.template: for great shall be the day of {0}

2.question: In Hosea 2:5, what did the harlot mother say her lovers gave her?
options: Her bread, water, wool, flax, oil, and drink

2.title: Gifts credited to Baal
2.text: Verse 8 is the heart of the offence: the corn, wine, oil, silver, and gold that the Lord himself gave were taken and 'prepared for Baal' — Israel thanked an idol for God's own gifts.

2.question: In Hosea 2:9, what did the Lord say he would take away again?
options: His corn and his wine, his wool and his flax

2.question: What did the Lord say he would cause to cease in Hosea 2:11?
options: Her mirth, her feast days, her new moons, and her sabbaths

2.question: In Hosea 2:23, what does the Lord promise to say to those who 'were not my people'?

3.title: Bought back for silver and barley
3.text: Hosea redeems his wife for 'fifteen pieces of silver, and for an homer of barley, and an half homer of barley' — a modest price that dramatizes how the Lord buys back a people who had wandered.

3.question: What does Hosea 3:5 say the children of Israel shall afterward do?
options: Return, and seek the Lord their God, and David their king
Build a new temple to Baal

4.title: The Lord's controversy
4.text: Hosea 4 opens like a courtroom: the Lord 'hath a controversy with the inhabitants of the land' — a legal charge that there is 'no truth, nor mercy, nor knowledge of God in the land.'

4.question: According to Hosea 4:1, what three things are lacking in the land?
options: Truth, mercy, and knowledge of God

4.question: Complete Hosea 4:6: 'My people are destroyed for lack of ___.'
What does Hosea 4:11 say takes away the heart?
options: Whoredom and wine and new wine

4.question: In Hosea 4:17, what does the Lord say of Ephraim, joined to idols?

5.title: A charge against priests, people, and king
5.text: Hosea 5 opens by summoning three parties at once — the priests, the house of Israel, and the house of the king — showing that the coming judgment spared no level of the nation.

5.question: According to Hosea 5:5, who shall fall together with Israel and Ephraim in their iniquity?
In Hosea 5:6, what happens when they go with flocks and herds to seek the Lord?
options: They shall not find him; he hath withdrawn himself

5.question: In Hosea 5:13, where did Ephraim go when he saw his sickness?
options: To the Assyrian, and sent to king Jareb

6.question: In Hosea 6:1, why do the people say they should return to the Lord?
options: He hath torn, and he will heal us; he hath smitten, and he will bind us up

6.title: Mercy, not sacrifice
6.text: Hosea 6:6 — 'I desired mercy, and not sacrifice' — is quoted twice by Jesus in Matthew, teaching that God prizes a compassionate heart over mere ritual offerings.

6.question: Complete Hosea 6:6: 'For I desired mercy, and not ___.'

7.title: The oven and the baker
7.text: Hosea repeatedly compares the people's inflamed passions to a baker's oven — their hearts kept hot with intrigue and adultery, blazing 'as a flaming fire' in the morning.

7.question: In Hosea 7:3, with what do the people make the king glad?
options: Their wickedness, and the princes with their lies

7.question: In Hosea 7:8, Ephraim is compared to a cake that is what?
In Hosea 7:11, to which two nations do the people of Ephraim turn like a silly dove?

8.title: Set the trumpet to thy mouth
8.text: Chapter 8 begins with an alarm: 'Set the trumpet to thy mouth.' The enemy would sweep down 'as an eagle' against the house of the Lord because Israel had broken the covenant.

8.question: In Hosea 8:4, what did Israel make of their silver and their gold?
options: Idols, that they may be cut off

8.question: Complete Hosea 8:7: 'For they have sown the wind, and they shall reap the ___.'
In Hosea 8:5, what is said of the calf of Samaria?
options: It shall be broken in pieces
It shall be carried to Egypt

9.title: No joy in the harvest
9.text: Chapter 9 turns the joy of the threshing floor into mourning: because Israel loved 'a reward upon every cornfloor' — pagan harvest revelry — the floor and winepress would fail them.

9.question: In Hosea 9:3, where does the prophet say Ephraim shall return?
In Hosea 9:7, how is the prophet described in that day of visitation?
In Hosea 9:15, because of the wickedness in Gilgal, what does the Lord say he will do?
options: Drive them out of his house and love them no more

10.title: An empty vine
10.text: Israel is called 'an empty vine' that bears fruit only for itself: the more it prospered, the more altars and images it built — abundance feeding idolatry rather than gratitude.

10.question: In Hosea 10:3, what will the people say, because they feared not the Lord?
In Hosea 10:6, where shall the calf be carried as a present to king Jareb?
In Hosea 10:13, what does the Lord say they have reaped, having plowed wickedness?

11.question: Complete Hosea 11:1: 'When Israel was a child, then I loved him, and called my son out of ___.'

11.title: A father teaching a child to walk
11.text: Verse 3 pictures the Lord tenderly teaching Ephraim 'to go, taking them by their arms' — one of scripture's most intimate images of God as a patient, loving parent.

11.question: In Hosea 11:5, who does the prophet say shall be Israel's king because they refused to return?
In Hosea 11:9, why does the Lord say he will not execute the fierceness of his anger?
options: For he is God, and not man; the Holy One in the midst of thee

12.title: Jacob, who took the heel
12.text: Hosea 12 reaches back to the patriarch: Jacob 'took his brother by the heel in the womb' and later wrestled and 'had power with God' — a mirror held up to his descendant nation, Israel.

12.question: In Hosea 12:2, with whom does the Lord say he has a controversy?
In Hosea 12:6, what does the prophet urge the people to do?
options: Turn to their God, keep mercy and judgment, and wait on their God

12.question: In Hosea 12:13, how did the Lord bring Israel out of Egypt?

13.title: Fleeting as morning cloud
13.text: Hosea returns to a favorite image: those who trust idols shall be 'as the morning cloud, and as the early dew that passeth away' — here also compared to chaff and to smoke out of the chimney.

13.question: In Hosea 13:4, what does the Lord say of himself, from the land of Egypt?
options: There is no saviour beside me
He is one of many gods

13.question: In Hosea 13:8, to what animal, bereaved of her whelps, does the Lord compare himself?
Complete Hosea 13:14: 'O death, I will be thy ___; O grave, I will be thy destruction.'

14.question: How does Hosea 14:1 begin its closing call to Israel?
options: O Israel, return unto the Lord thy God
O Israel, seek out a king

14.title: I will love them freely
14.text: The book ends on grace: 'I will heal their backsliding, I will love them freely' — after all the judgment, the final word of Hosea is the Lord's freely given, unearned love.

14.question: In Hosea 14:9, what is said of the ways of the Lord?
options: They are right, and the just shall walk in them
